title: System and method for an expandable landing locking shoulder
abstract: A system for suspending a hanger (40) within a wellbore component (12) includes an actuation ring (82) circumferentially positioned about the hanger (40). The system also includes an arm (78) removably coupled to the actuation ring (82), the arm (78) extending longitudinally from the actuation ring (82) and including a head (116). The system further includes a landing profile (112) formed on the head (116), the landing profile (112) including a plurality of landing features (114). The system includes a tag shoulder (130) formed on the actuation ring (82), the tag shoulder (130) arranged to contact a protrusion (184) within the wellbore component (12) to drive upward axial movement of the actuation ring (82) along an axis (64), the upward axial movement being transferred to the arm (78) to move the arm (78) toward an activated position.
claims:
Claims 1. A system for supporting a wellbore tubular within a wellbore, the system comprising: a wellbore component (12) associated with the wellbore, the wellbore component comprising an axial bore (18) arranged along an axis (64); a hanger (40) installed within the wellbore component to support the wellbore tubular, the hanger (40) comprising a shoulder (56) that receives the wellbore tubular and suspends the wellbore tubular along the axis (64); and an expandable landing locking shoulder (76) arranged circumferentially about the hanger (40), the expandable landing locking shoulder (76) comprising: an actuation ring (82) removably coupled to a body (58) of the hanger (40), the actuation ring (82) comprising a shoulder (92) and an extension (102), the extension (102) extending longitudinally in an upward direction from the shoulder (92); a body portion (80) coupled to the actuation ring (82), the body portion (80) comprising coupling members (84) that mate with corresponding coupling members (88) of the actuation ring (82); and arms (78) extending in the upward direction from the body portion (80), the arms (78) comprising a head (116) with a landing profile (112) that engage a recess (182) formed in the wellbore component (12) when in an activated position. 2. The system of claim 2, further comprising: 

 an activation shoulder (184) formed in the wellbore component (12), the activation shoulder (184) protruding radially into the axial bore (18); and a tag shoulder (130) formed on the actuation ring (82), wherein the tag shoulder (130) contacts the activation shoulder (184) when the hanger (40) is installed within the wellbore component (12), the tag shoulder (130) transmitting an upward force from the activation shoulder (184) to drive the actuation ring (82) longitudinally uphole along the axis (64) to transition the expandable landing locking shoulder (76) from a deactivated position to the activated position. 3. The system of claim 1, further comprising: voids (152) positioned between adjacent arms (78) of the expandable landing locking shoulder (76), the voids (152) separating the arms (78) to enable outward radial flexion of the arms (78), wherein the voids (152) form a flow path between the hanger (40) and the axial bore (18) to enable flow by during installation of the hanger (40). 4. The system of claim 1, further comprising: a shear pin (94) extending through the actuation ring (82), the shear pin (94) coupling the actuation ring (82) to the hanger (40) via respective apertures (134, 136) formed in the actuation ring (82) and the hanger (40), wherein the shear pin (94) holds the actuation ring (82) in a predetermined position until the actuation ring (82) is transitioned to the activated position. 5. The system of claim 1, further comprising: 

 a seal (72) circumferentially about an upper portion (74) of the hanger (40), the seal (72) forming a fluid barrier between the hanger (40) and the axial bore (18) when the hanger (40) is in the activated position. 6. The system of claim 1, further comprising: a tapered shoulder (60) extending along the hanger (40), the tapered shoulder (60) having a downward angle that extends from an outer diameter (66) of a first portion of the hanger (40) toward the axis (64), wherein at least the head (116) of the expandable landing locking shoulder (76) is driven radially outward via the tapered shoulder (60) as the actuation ring (82) moves upward along the axis (64) toward the activated position. 7. The system of claim 1, further comprising: a stop shoulder (100) formed on the hanger (40), the stop shoulder (40) blocking upward movement of the actuation ring (82) via contact with the extension (102), the stop shoulder (100) limiting upward axial movement of the actuation ring (82) beyond the activation position. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein the landing profile (112) comprises a plurality of landing shoulders (150), the landing shoulders (150) extending into the recess (182) and engaging the wellbore component (12) to secure the hanger (40) within the wellbore component (12). 9. A system for suspending a hanger (40) within a wellbore component (12), the system comprising: 

 an actuation ring (82) circumferentially positioned about the hanger (40), the actuation ring (82) having a substantially annular shape; an arm (78) removably coupled to the actuation ring (82), the arm (82) extending longitudinally from the actuation ring (82) and comprising a head (116) at an end opposite the actuation ring (82); a landing profile (112) formed on the head (116) on an outer diameter of the head (116), the landing profile (112) comprising a plurality of landing features (114) forming a plurality of landing shoulders (150); and a tag shoulder (130) formed on the actuation ring (82), the tag shoulder (130) arranged to contact a protrusion (184) within the wellbore component (12) to drive upward axial movement of the actuation ring (82) along an axis (64), the upward axial movement being transferred to the arm (78) to move the arm (78) toward an activated position. 10. The system of claim 9, wherein the hanger (40) comprises a tapered shoulder (60) and the head (116) is arranged along the tapered shoulder (60), the head (116) being driven along the tapered shoulder (60) via movement of the actuation ring (82) to drive the head (116) radially outward from the axis (64). 11. The system of claim 9, wherein the arm (78) comprises a plurality of arms (78), the system further comprising: 

 a void (152) arranged between adjacent arms (78) of the plurality of arms (78), the void (152) having a width (156) less than an arm width (154) and a curvature (164) at a bottom thereof, the void (152) forming a flow path for circulating fluid. 12. The system of claim 11, wherein a length (158) of the void (152) is greater than a length of the head (116), the void (152) extending from a top of the arm (78) to a body portion (80) that couples the arm (78) to the actuation ring (82). 13. The system of claim 9, wherein the hanger (40) comprises a stop shoulder (100), the system further comprising: a gap (96) formed between an extension (102) extending longitudinally from the actuation ring (82) and the stop shoulder (100), the gap (96) limiting a distance of movement of the actuation ring (82) along the hanger (40). 14. The system of claim 9, further comprising: an aperture (134) formed through the arm (78), the aperture (134) receiving a shear pin (94) to couple the actuation ring (82) to the hanger (40) at a predetermined position. 15. The system of claim 9, wherein the arms (78) are formed from a metal, a plastic, a composite material, or a combination thereof.
